message:
fix(engine): expand rewrite pack and interference metadata hash coverage

The versioned rewrite pack content hash only covered pack_id, version,
epoch, and each rule's ID/pattern_hash/replacement_hash — omitting
description, cost_model_id, interference hash, rule categories,
descriptions, proven_sound flags, priorities, cost classes, and enabled
flags. Similarly, the interference metadata hash used raw enum
discriminant bytes instead of stable string representations, and
omitted the detail field entirely.

Changes to RewritePack::compute_hash():
- Add description, cost_model_id, and interference content_hash to the
  hash preimage
- Add per-rule: category (as string), description, proven_sound flag,
  priority_millionths, affected_cost_classes (as strings), enabled flag
- Expand function signature to accept the additional inputs

Changes to InterferenceMetadata::compute_hash():
- Replace `[entry.kind as u8]` with `entry.kind.to_string().as_bytes()`
  for stable cross-version hashing (enum reordering no longer breaks
  hash continuity)
- Add `entry.detail.as_bytes()` to the hash preimage

Changes to DeterministicCostModel::compute_hash():
- Replace `[*class as u8]` with `class.to_string().as_bytes()` for the
  same stability reason

Tests:
- interference_metadata_detail_changes_hash: verify detail field
  mutation changes the hash
- Additional integration tests for expanded hash coverage

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
